topic: The houses on my street go from forty seven to forty nine and nobody remembers a forty eight ever existing
title: Houses on My Street Skip from 47 to 49 and Nobody Remembers a 48 Ever Existing | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: Houses on my block jump from forty seven to forty nine. Nobody remembers a forty eight.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a residential cul de sac at dusk, five houses visible with numbered mailboxes, the lawn between two houses notably empty, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Quiet cul de sac. Five houses. I have lived here twelve years.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior of a quiet suburban cul de sac in mid afternoon, neat lawns and brick mailboxes, no people or cars, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Forty five. Forty seven. Forty nine. Fifty one.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of three mailboxes side by side on metal posts, numbered forty five, forty seven, and forty nine, dim ambient daylight, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Property tax bill came addressed to forty eight Hawthorn Street.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an opened envelope with a property tax assessment letter on a kitchen counter, the address line showing 48 Hawthorn Street, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Took it to the assessor. Asked them.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a county assessor office desk, a clerk seen from behind, a thick paper records book open, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: She found a record. Forty eight Hawthorn was built nineteen sixty two.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an aged property record book page showing typewritten entries with a nineteen sixty two construction date for forty eight Hawthorn, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Drove home. Walked the block. Counted.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a person's lower legs walking along a residential sidewalk past mailboxes in afternoon light, no face visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Five houses. No forty eight. Empty lawn between forty seven and forty nine.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of two adjacent residential lots side by side, one with a house and one with only mowed grass and no driveway, late afternoon light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Asked the neighbors. None of them remembered a house ever standing there.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of two neighbors talking over a fence in afternoon light, both seen from behind, no faces visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Old aerial photos show a sixth house. The space between is empty grass now.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an old black and white aerial photograph of a residential block, six houses clearly visible, an arrow drawn on the photo pointing to one, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last week the mail carrier knocked on my door.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior of a suburban front porch with a person in a postal service uniform seen from behind knocking on the door, a parcel in their other hand, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: He had a package addressed to forty eight Hawthorn. He asked who lived there.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a cardboard package addressed in marker to 48 Hawthorn Street sitting on a wooden porch railing, no people visible, vertical 9:16 framing
